description: English localized v2 texts for orientation-aware rules.

general.text: The card is upright, so its message may be easier to meet directly in the outer situation.
general.advice: Work with the card plainly: what quality is it asking you to notice, practice, or receive?
general.journalPrompt: Where is this card's energy already visible in my life?
general.challenge: A clear message can still be overlooked if it feels too simple.
general.opportunity: Direct reflection can lead to a grounded next step.

general.text: The card is reversed, so its message may be internal, delayed, blocked, or asking for gentler attention.
general.advice: Read the reversal as an invitation to adjust and listen, not as a negative verdict.
general.journalPrompt: Where is this energy asking for care, honesty, or release inside me?
general.challenge: You may be tempted to treat delay or blockage as failure.
general.opportunity: Inner work can make the outer step clearer.

general.text: All three cards are upright, giving the reading a feeling of directness, visibility, and available movement.
general.advice: Let the message be simple without making it shallow; choose one conscious action that honors the flow.
general.journalPrompt: What feels ready to move from reflection into practice?
general.challenge: Flowing energy may tempt you to move faster than awareness.
general.opportunity: The path may be easier to engage when you stay present.

general.text: All three cards are reversed, turning the spread toward hidden, delayed, tender, or unfinished inner material.
general.advice: Slow down and ask what needs repair, rest, honesty, or integration before it becomes outward action.
general.journalPrompt: What is not ready to move outward because it still needs inner care?
general.challenge: The reading may feel heavy if reversals are treated as bad signs.
general.opportunity: Deep inner adjustment can become quiet liberation.

general.text: A reversed past card followed by an upright present card suggests that a past blockage may be loosening in the current moment.
general.advice: Notice what is more available now than it used to be, and do not let old timing define the present.
general.journalPrompt: What old blockage is no longer as powerful as it once was?
general.challenge: Memory may insist nothing has changed.
general.opportunity: The present can become a doorway out of an old pattern.

general.text: A reversed present card with an upright future card suggests that the current blockage may become workable if it is met honestly.
general.advice: Give attention to the present friction; the future opens through integration, not denial.
general.journalPrompt: What needs care now so the next step can become freer?
general.challenge: It may be tempting to skip over the present discomfort.
general.opportunity: Careful attention now can make later movement cleaner.

general.theme: A future energy asking for care
general.text: A reversed future card does not predict failure; it highlights an energy that may need attention before it can express well.
general.advice: Ask what preparation, boundary, repair, or grounding would help that future energy unfold more cleanly.
general.journalPrompt: What can I tend now so this possible future feels less blocked?
general.challenge: You may read caution as certainty.
general.opportunity: Awareness now can change how the path is met.

general.text: An upright past followed by a reversed present suggests that something once clear may now need review, adjustment, or renewed honesty.
general.advice: Do not assume the old method still fits; ask what has changed in the current energy.
general.journalPrompt: What used to work that now needs a different approach?
general.challenge: Nostalgia may make the present harder to see clearly.
general.opportunity: A timely adjustment can protect what still matters.

general.theme: One light in the inward work
general.text: Two reversed cards and one upright card suggest that much of the reading is inward, but one energy may already be available as support.
general.advice: Find the upright card and let it become the handle for working with the more tender material.
general.journalPrompt: Which part of this reading feels available enough to support the rest?
general.challenge: The reversed cards may draw all your attention.
general.opportunity: One clear resource can help unlock the blocked places.

general.theme: One tender point in a flowing spread
general.text: One reversed card among two upright cards suggests a mostly available path with one area needing extra care.
general.advice: Do not let one tender point cancel the whole reading; let it show where refinement is needed.
general.journalPrompt: Which part of the path is flowing, and which part asks for gentleness?
general.challenge: A single blockage may feel larger than it is.
general.opportunity: Focused care can support the whole spread.

general.text: The spread begins reversed and then turns upright, suggesting a movement from inward blockage toward clearer engagement.
general.advice: Honor the starting difficulty, then notice how the later cards invite participation.
general.journalPrompt: What helps me move from internal resistance toward available action?
general.challenge: The first blockage may color the whole story.
general.opportunity: The path can become lighter as the energy is worked with.

general.text: An upright-reversed-upright pattern suggests that the center of the reading holds the main adjustment point.
general.advice: Treat the present card as the hinge: what needs to be understood, softened, or corrected now?
general.journalPrompt: What present adjustment would help the whole story flow better?
general.challenge: The beginning and future may distract from the current work.
general.opportunity: Caring for the middle can align the path.

general.text: Two upright cards followed by a reversed future card suggest that the path has available energy, but its next expression may need preparation.
general.advice: Let momentum continue, while strengthening the area that could become blocked or unbalanced.
general.journalPrompt: What future complication can I prepare for with calm awareness?
general.challenge: Early flow may make preparation seem unnecessary.
general.opportunity: Gentle caution can protect the progress already made.

general.text: When a matched pair is both reversed, the connection between them may be private, delayed, blocked, or asking for repair.
general.advice: Read the pair as a knot to loosen, not as a sentence to accept.
general.journalPrompt: What does this pair need before its energy can move honestly?
general.challenge: Double reversal may feel like stuckness.
general.opportunity: A careful adjustment can unlock the relationship between the cards.

general.text: A reversed card moving into an upright card suggests a shift from inner work toward clearer expression.
general.advice: Ask what the first card must release or integrate so the second card can be lived more fully.
general.journalPrompt: What hidden work supports the clearer energy that follows?
general.challenge: The transition may be missed if you focus only on the blockage.
general.opportunity: A blocked place can become the source of movement.

career.challenge: The transition may be missed if you focus only on the blockage.
career.opportunity: A blocked place can become the source of movement.

money.text: A reversed card moving into an upright card suggests a shift from inner work toward clearer expression.
money.advice: Ask what the first card must release or integrate so the second card can be lived more fully.
money.journalPrompt: What hidden work supports the clearer energy that follows?
money.challenge: The transition may be missed if you focus only on the blockage.
money.opportunity: A blocked place can become the source of movement.

growth.challenge: The transition may be missed if you focus only on the blockage.

general.text: An upright card moving into a reversed card suggests that available energy may need adjustment before it continues.
general.advice: Do not abandon the first card's gift; refine how it is being carried forward.
general.journalPrompt: What good energy needs better timing, boundaries, or grounding?
general.challenge: A later reversal may create discouragement.
general.opportunity: Refinement can preserve what is valuable.

general.text: The alternating pattern shows movement interrupted by reflection, as if the path is asking you to pause in the middle before continuing.
general.advice: Trust the rhythm: action, adjustment, action can be wiser than constant speed.
general.journalPrompt: Where does a pause improve the movement rather than stop it?
general.challenge: The pause may feel like contradiction.
general.opportunity: Rhythm can make progress more sustainable.

general.text: With no reversals present, the reading may be offering its message in a relatively direct and approachable way.
general.advice: Begin with the upright meanings, then let your intuition add nuance without overcomplicating the message.
general.journalPrompt: What is the simplest honest message here?
general.challenge: Simplicity may be mistaken for lack of depth.
general.opportunity: A clear message can be easier to integrate.
